title: Multilayer coating
abstract: The present invention relates to multilayer insulating coatings for electrical conductors and a method of formation thereof. In particular, the invention relates to multilayer insulating coatings for electrical conductors used in high temperature applications.
claims:
Claims 1. An electrical conductor coated with a multilayer coating; wherein the multilayer coating comprises: a continuous layer of polymeric material; a continuous layer of ceramic material; and at least one additional continuous layer of polymeric material. 2. A conductor according to claim 1 , wherein the continuous layer of ceramic material is situated between two continuous layers of polymeric material. 3. A conductor according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein each continuous layer of polymeric material comprises a polyimide. 4. A conductor according to claim 1 , wherein the coating further comprises at least one additional continuous layer of ceramic material. 5. A conductor according to any preceding claim, wherein each continuous layer of ceramic material comprises: an inorganic oxide network, wherein at least a portion of the inorganic oxide network comprises organic moieties that are covalently bonded to the inorganic oxide. 6. A conductor according to claim 5, wherein the inorganic oxide polymer is selected from silica, alumina and zirconia; and wherein the organic moiety is independently at each occurrence selected from an alkyl (e.g. methyl), phenyl, primary or secondary amine containing group, and epoxide-containing group. 7. A conductor according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the innermost layer of the multilayer coating is a layer of polymeric material. 8. A conductor according to any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the outermost layer of the multilayer coating is a layer of polymeric material. 9. A conductor according to any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the coating comprises an intermediate layer disposed between a continuous layer of ceramic material and a continuous layer of polymeric material, said intermediate layer comprising a hybrid organic-ceramic nanocomposite material. 10. A conductor according to claim 9, wherein an intermediate layer is disposed at every interface between a continuous layer of ceramic material and a continuous layer of polymeric material. 11. A conductor according to any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the continuous polymeric layer, the intermediate layer, and/or the continuous ceramic layer are functionalised to increase the affinity between the neighbouring layers. 12. A conductor of any preceding claim, wherein the electrical conductor is a metal. 13. A conductor of claim 12, wherein the electrical conductor is a metal wire. 14. A conductor according to any preceding claim, wherein the thickness of each continuous layer of polymeric material is in the range from 1 pm to 15 pm. 15. A conductor according to any preceding claim, wherein the thickness of each continuous layer of ceramic material is in the range from 0.5 pm to 8 pm. 16. A conductor according to any preceding claim, wherein the multilayer coating has a total thickness in the range from 10 pm to 100 pm. 17. A method of producing a coated electrical conductor, the method comprising: providing a multilayer coating on an electrical conductor, wherein the multilayer coating comprises: a continuous layer of polymeric material; a continuous layer of ceramic material; and at least one additional continuous layer of polymeric material. 18. A method of claim 17, wherein the coated electrical conductor is an electrical conductor of any one of claims 1 to 16. 19. The method of claim 17 or claim 18, wherein each layer of the multilayer coating is applied in a separate process step. 20. The method of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein the method comprises coating the electrical conductor with a first continuous layer of polymeric material, coating said first continuous layer of polymeric material with a continuous layer of ceramic material, and then coating said continuous layer of ceramic material with a second continuous layer of polymeric material. 21. The method of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein the method comprises coating the electrical conductor with a first continuous layer of polymeric material, coating said first continuous layer of polymeric material with a first intermediate layer of a 

 hybrid material, coating said first intermediate layer with a continuous layer of a ceramic material, coating said continuous layer of polymeric material with a second intermediate layer of a hybrid material and coating said second intermediate layer with a second continuous layer of a polymeric material. 22. The method of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein the continuous ceramic layer or each continuous ceramic layer is formed by extruding a paste comprising the components from which the coating is formed onto the conductor (or onto a previously applied layer of coating). 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the components from which the ceramic material is formed comprise a trialkylalkoxysilane, e.g. trimethylmethoxysilane. 24. The method of claim 22 or claim 23, wherein the paste comprises a particulate ceramic.
